content.text: I don't have a direct "GTM" (Google Tag Manager) integration available. Let me check what functions are currently connected to see if there's anything related.

/**
 *
 */
namespace Analyze {
  /**
   * Analyze code structure in 3 modes: 1) Directory overview - file tree with LOC/function/class counts to max_depth. 2) File details - functions, classes, imports. 3) Symbol focus - call graphs across directory to max_depth (requires file or directory path, case-sensitive). Typical flow: directory → files → symbols. Functions called >3x show •N.
   */
  export async function analyze(input: AnalyzeInput): Promise<any>
}

/**
 *
 */
namespace Skills {
  /**
   * Load a skill's full content into your context so you can follow its instructions.
   *
   * Skills are listed in your system instructions. When you need to use one, load it first to get the detailed instructions.
   *
   * Examples:
   * - load_skill(name: "gdrive") → Loads the gdrive skill instructions
   * - load_skill(name: "my-skill/template.md") → Loads a supporting file
   */
  export async function loadSkill(input: LoadSkillInput): Promise<any>
}

/**
 *
 */
namespace Todo {
  /**
   * Overwrite the entire TODO content.
   *
   * The content persists across conversation turns and compaction. Use this for:
   * - Task tracking and progress updates
   * - Important notes and reminders
   *
   * WARNING: This operation completely replaces the existing content. Always include
   * all content you want to keep, not just the changes.
   *
   */
  export async function todoWrite(input: TodoWriteInput): Promise<any>
}

/**
 *
 */
namespace Summon {
  /**
   * Load knowledge into your current context or discover available sources.
   *
   * Call with no arguments to list all available sources (subrecipes, recipes, agents).
   * Call with a source name to load its content into your context.
   * For background tasks: load(source: "task_id") waits for the task and returns the result.
   * To cancel a running task: load(source: "task_id", cancel: true) stops and returns output.
   *
   * Examples:
   * - load() → Lists available sources
   * - load(source: "deploy") → Loads the deploy recipe
   * - load(source: "20260219_1") → Waits for background task, then returns result
   */
  export async function load(input: LoadInput = {}): Promise<any>

  /**
   * Delegate a task to a subagent that runs independently with its own context.
   *
   * Modes:
   * 1. Ad-hoc: Provide `instructions` for a custom task
   * 2. Source-based: Provide `source` name to run a subrecipe, recipe, or agent
   * 3. Combined: Pair a source with a task (e.g., source: "deploy", instructions: "deploy to staging")
   *
   * Effective Delegation:
   * - Delegates know only instructions + source content
   * - Delegates cannot coordinate. Same-file work = conflicts.
   * - Parallel: async: true, then load(taskId) to wait and get results. Single: sync.
   *
   * Research (read-only): parallelize freely - delegates explore and report back.
   * Work (writes): partition files strictly - no two delegates touch the same file.
   *
   * Decompose → async delegates → load(taskId) for each → synthesize.
   */
  export async function delegate(input: DelegateInput = {}): Promise<any>
}

/**
 *
 */
namespace Apps {
  /**
   * List all available Goose apps with their names and descriptions. Use this to see what apps exist before creating or modifying apps.
   */
  export async function listApps(input: { [key: string]: any | undefined } = {}): Promise<any>

  /**
   * Create a new Goose app based on a description or PRD. The extension will use an LLM to generate the HTML/CSS/JavaScript. Apps are sandboxed and run in standalone windows.
   */
  export async function createApp(input: CreateAppInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * Improve an existing app based on feedback. The extension will use an LLM to update the HTML while preserving the app's intent.
   */
  export async function iterateApp(input: IterateAppInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * Delete an app permanently
   */
  export async function deleteApp(input: DeleteAppInput): Promise<any>
}

/**
 *
 */
namespace Autovisualiser {
  /**
   * Show a chord diagram visualization for showing relationships and flows between entities.
   *
   * The data must contain:
   * - labels: Array of strings representing the entities
   * - matrix: 2D array of numbers representing flows (matrix[i][j] = flow from i to j)
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "labels": ["North America", "Europe", "Asia", "Africa"],
   *   "matrix": [
   *     [0, 15, 25, 8],
   *     [18, 0, 20, 12],
   *     [22, 18, 0, 15],
   *     [5, 10, 18, 0]
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function renderChord(input: RenderChordInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show pie or donut charts for categorical data visualization.
   * Supports one or more charts in a grid layout.
   * The `data` field must always be an array; pass a single-element array for one chart.
   *
   * Each chart object must contain:
   * - values: Array of numbers OR objects with 'label' and 'value'
   * - type: Optional 'doughnut' (default) or 'pie'
   * - title: Optional chart title
   * - labels: Optional array of labels (required when values are plain numbers)
   *
   * Example single chart (labeled values):
   * [
   *   {
   *     "values": [
   *       {"label": "Marketing", "value": 25000},
   *       {"label": "Development", "value": 35000}
   *     ],
   *     "title": "Budget"
   *   }
   * ]
   *
   * Example single chart (parallel arrays):
   * [
   *   {
   *     "values": [45000, 38000],
   *     "labels": ["Product A", "Product B"],
   *     "type": "pie"
   *   }
   * ]
   *
   * Example multiple charts (array of chart objects):
   * [
   *   {"values": [60, 40], "labels": ["Yes", "No"], "title": "Q1"},
   *   {"values": [75, 25], "labels": ["Yes", "No"], "title": "Q2"}
   * ]
   */
  export async function renderDonut(input: RenderDonutInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show an interactive map visualization with location markers using Leaflet.
   *
   * The data must contain:
   * - markers: Array of objects with 'lat', 'lng', and optional properties
   * - title: Optional title for the map (default: "Interactive Map")
   * - subtitle: Optional subtitle (default: "Geographic data visualization")
   * - center: Optional center point {lat, lng} (default: USA center)
   * - zoom: Optional initial zoom level (default: 4)
   * - clustering: Optional boolean to enable/disable clustering (default: true)
   * - autoFit: Optional boolean to auto-fit map to markers (default: true)
   *
   * Marker properties:
   * - lat: Latitude (required)
   * - lng: Longitude (required)
   * - name: Location name
   * - value: Numeric value for sizing/coloring
   * - description: Description text
   * - popup: Custom popup HTML
   * - color: Custom marker color
   * - label: Custom marker label
   * - useDefaultIcon: Use default Leaflet icon
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "title": "Store Locations",
   *   "markers": [
   *     {"lat": 37.7749, "lng": -122.4194, "name": "SF Store", "value": 150000},
   *     {"lat": 40.7128, "lng": -74.0060, "name": "NYC Store", "value": 200000}
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function renderMap(input: RenderMapInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show a Mermaid diagram from Mermaid syntax
   *
   * Provide the Mermaid code as a string. Supports flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, etc.
   *
   * Example:
   * graph TD;
   *     A-->B;
   *     A-->C;
   *     B-->D;
   *     C-->D;
   *
   */
  export async function renderMermaid(input: RenderMermaidInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show a radar chart (spider chart) for multi-dimensional data comparison
   *
   * The data must contain:
   * - labels: Array of strings representing the dimensions/axes
   * - datasets: Array of dataset objects with 'label' and 'data' properties
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "labels": ["Speed", "Strength", "Endurance", "Agility", "Intelligence"],
   *   "datasets": [
   *     {
   *       "label": "Player 1",
   *       "data": [85, 70, 90, 75, 80]
   *     },
   *     {
   *       "label": "Player 2",
   *       "data": [75, 85, 80, 90, 70]
   *     }
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function renderRadar(input: RenderRadarInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show a Sankey diagram from flow data
   * The data must contain:
   * - nodes: Array of objects with 'name' and optional 'category' properties
   * - links: Array of objects with 'source', 'target', and 'value' properties
   *
   * IMPORTANT: Links must NOT form cycles (e.g. A→B and B→A). Sankey diagrams are
   * directional acyclic flows. If the data has circular relationships, restructure
   * them so flow moves in one direction (e.g. add a separate node like "Re-signed"
   * instead of linking back to an earlier node).
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "nodes": [
   *     {"name": "Source A", "category": "source"},
   *     {"name": "Target B", "category": "target"}
   *   ],
   *   "links": [
   *     {"source": "Source A", "target": "Target B", "value": 100}
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function renderSankey(input: RenderSankeyInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show a treemap visualization for hierarchical data with proportional area representation as boxes
   *
   * The data should be a hierarchical structure with:
   * - name: Name of the node (required)
   * - value: Numeric value for leaf nodes (optional for parent nodes)
   * - children: Array of child nodes (optional)
   * - category: Category for coloring (optional)
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "name": "Root",
   *   "children": [
   *     {
   *       "name": "Group A",
   *       "children": [
   *         {"name": "Item 1", "value": 100, "category": "Type1"},
   *         {"name": "Item 2", "value": 200, "category": "Type2"}
   *       ]
   *     },
   *     {"name": "Item 3", "value": 150, "category": "Type1"}
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function renderTreemap(input: RenderTreemapInput): Promise<any>

  /**
   * show interactive line, scatter, or bar charts
   *
   * Required: type ('line', 'scatter', or 'bar'), datasets array
   * Optional: labels, title, subtitle, xAxisLabel, yAxisLabel, options
   *
   * Example:
   * {
   *   "type": "line",
   *   "title": "Monthly Sales",
   *   "labels": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"],
   *   "datasets": [
   *     {"label": "Product A", "data": [65, 59, 80]}
   *   ]
   * }
   */
  export async function showChart(input: ShowChartInput): Promise<any>
}

No, I'm **not connected to GTM** (Google Tag Manager). 

The tools I currently have access to are:
- **Analyze** – code structure analysis
- **Skills / Summon** – load skills, recipes, agents, or delegate tasks
- **Todo** – task tracking
- **Apps** – create/manage Goose apps
- **Autovisualiser** – charts, maps, diagrams (chord, donut, map, mermaid, radar, sankey, treemap, line/bar/scatter)

If you want to connect to Google Tag Manager, you'd need to add an extension/MCP server that provides GTM API access. Want help finding or setting one up, or did you mean something else by "GTM"?
